title: Helenium × strictum
taxonomy: Helenium × strictum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and relationships with other plant species. Helenium × strictum is further classified within the genus Helenium, which comprises a group of flowering plants commonly known as sneezeweed. Within the genus, Helenium × strictum is a hybrid species, resulting from the cross-breeding of other Helenium species. This hybrid classification is often denoted by the '×' symbol, indicating its inter-specific origin.
uses: Helenium × strictum, also known as strict sneezeweed, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and flowers are used to create infusions, tinctures, and salves for various health issues. Some of its medicinal uses include treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems such as colds and flu. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's effectiveness and safety for human consumption.

intro: Helenium × strictum, also known as Sneezeweed, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and ability to grow in a variety of moist environments. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in reducing pain and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Helenium × strictum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health ailments.
